top_pain_points.signal: Meal planning is time-consuming, mentally draining, and creates decision fatigue, especially for busy families or individuals with ADHD.
Creating and managing grocery lists is a tedious, error-prone process that leads to inefficient shopping and food waste.
Finding recipes that are both simple and flavorful, while also meeting specific dietary preferences (low-carb, protein-dense) or restrictions, is frustrating.
Online grocery shopping is fragmented; transferring a list manually or dealing with item mismatches/availability errors in app integrations is a major hassle.
Existing recipe apps or meal planners have poor UI, confusing navigation, or lack critical features like proper meal plan views, saving non-plan recipes, or sharing functionality.
Recipe instructions are often poorly written, with steps out of order, missing ingredient amounts (e.g., 'half a small bunch'), or overly basic descriptions.
The app itself has technical bugs: UI doesn't fit screens, crashes when modifying ingredients, fails to load new recipes, has a broken share function on Android.
Difficulty in customizing or importing personal recipes is time-consuming and frustrating, limiting flexibility.
Recipe variety is limited or new recipes are not updated quickly on the app compared to the website.
Hates wading through blog post recipes and cookbooks to find recipes
Frustrated with recipes never formatted the way they think
Dislikes the process of transferring recipes to a shopping list manually

top_desired_outcomes.signal: Save time on meal planning and grocery shopping

top_deal_breakers.signal: Core functionality (grocery list transfer, recipe access) is broken by persistent, unfixed bugs
Recipe quality is consistently poor or bland, making meals unenjoyable
The app cannot adapt to core dietary needs or household serving sizes
Grocery integration fails completely with the user's preferred retailer
Cannot accommodate specific dietary restrictions/allergies
No integration with preferred grocery services
Recipes are consistently bland or unappealing to family
Subscription cost outweighs savings from reduced food waste/takeout
App is buggy/unusable (text cut off, navigation broken)
Grocery list integration fails consistently

Need to solve a specific pain point like reducing food waste, cooking for dietary needs, or helping a large, busy family organize meals.
Realizing it can replace expensive meal kit services for less money
Finding the free version useful but wanting more features like nutrition info or adding own recipes
Seeing significant time savings in meal planning and grocery shopping

pain_points.signal: Frustration with account creation/login issues (password reset loops, location defaults, postal code format errors)
Anger at high, hidden, or unclear fees (Trust Fee, booking fees, VAT, 35-38% platform fee)

Wasted time due to no available Taskers or Taskers declining/redirecting without a saved task

Distrust in Tasker quality, pricing fairness, and potential scams (requests for cash, absurd prices, unqualified workers)
